seo.title: 100 Docker & Containers resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical Docker & Containers resources for engineers.

content.intro: This resource guide provides actionable patterns and tools for optimizing Docker workflows, focusing on building slim, secure images and managing complex local development environments with Docker Compose.

items.title: Implement Multi-Stage Builds
items.description: Separate build-time dependencies from the final runtime image using 'FROM ... AS build' and 'COPY --from=build' to reduce image size by up to 90%.

items.title: BuildKit Cache Mounts
items.description: Use 'RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm' to persist package manager caches between builds, significantly reducing CI/CD build times.

items.title: Distroless Runtime Images
items.description: Use GoogleContainerTools/distroless as the final stage base to remove shells and package managers, minimizing the attack surface.

items.title: Layer Ordering for Caching
items.description: Copy dependency files (package.json, go.mod) and run install commands before copying the rest of the source code to maximize layer reuse.

items.title: Hadolint for Linting
items.description: Integrate Hadolint into your CI pipeline to enforce Dockerfile best practices and catch inefficient instructions like 'RUN apt-get upgrade'.

items.title: Non-Root User Configuration
items.description: Explicitly create a system user and use the 'USER' instruction to avoid running container processes as root, preventing container breakout exploits.

items.title: Tini Init Process
items.description: Use 'tini' as your entrypoint to correctly handle signal forwarding and reap zombie processes in containers running complex applications.

items.title: .dockerignore Optimization
items.description: Exclude .git, node_modules, and local build artifacts to prevent sending unnecessary context to the Docker daemon and bloating layers.

items.title: Specific Tag Versioning
items.description: Avoid the ':latest' tag; use specific semantic versions or SHA256 hashes for base images to ensure build reproducibility across environments.

items.title: Dive Layer Inspection
items.description: Use the 'dive' CLI tool to analyze image layers and identify wasted space or files that should have been excluded from the final image.

items.title: Healthcheck-based Dependencies
items.description: Use 'depends_on' with 'condition: service_healthy' to ensure the application only starts after the database is ready to accept connections.

items.title: Docker Compose Profiles
items.description: Define 'profiles' in your YAML to selectively start services, such as 'debug' or 'testing' tools, without cluttering the default stack.

items.title: Bind Mounts for Hot Reload
items.description: Map local source directories to container paths using volumes to enable live-reloading frameworks (like Vite or Nodemon) without rebuilding.

items.title: Environment Variable Files
items.description: Utilize '.env' files alongside 'env_file' directives to manage secrets and configurations locally without hardcoding values in the YAML.

items.title: External Network Isolation
items.description: Define external networks to allow communication between separate Compose projects while keeping database traffic isolated from the public bridge.

items.title: Resource Constraints
items.description: Apply 'deploy.resources.limits' in Compose files to simulate production constraints (CPU/Memory) and prevent local resource exhaustion.

items.title: Traefik Reverse Proxy Integration
items.description: Run Traefik in a container to provide automatic SSL and local domain routing (e.g., app.localhost) for multiple Compose services.

items.title: Docker Compose Overrides
items.description: Use 'docker-compose.override.yml' for local-only settings like debug ports, keeping the base YAML clean for production-like environments.

items.title: Named Volumes for Persistence
items.description: Use named volumes instead of host paths for database data to improve I/O performance on macOS and Windows via VirtioFS.

items.title: Lazydocker TUI
items.description: Manage containers, logs, and resource usage from the terminal using 'lazydocker' for faster troubleshooting than the standard CLI.

items.title: Trivy Vulnerability Scanning
items.description: Run 'trivy image <name>' in your CI pipeline to detect CVEs in OS packages and application dependencies before deployment.

items.title: Read-Only Root Filesystem
items.description: Set 'read_only: true' in Compose or '--read-only' in CLI to prevent attackers from writing malicious scripts to the container's disk.

items.title: Log Rotation Configuration
items.description: Configure the 'json-file' logging driver with 'max-size' and 'max-file' limits to prevent container logs from consuming all host disk space.

items.title: Seccomp Profile Hardening
items.description: Apply custom Seccomp profiles to restrict the system calls a container can make, mitigating kernel-level exploits.

items.title: Docker Scout SBOM
items.description: Generate a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) using Docker Scout to track exactly which software versions are running in production.

items.title: Automated Image Pruning
items.description: Schedule 'docker system prune -af --volumes' via cron to remove dangling images and unused networks that accumulate on build nodes.

items.title: GHCR Authenticated Pulls
items.description: Configure GitHub Actions to use 'docker/login-action' with temporary GITHUB_TOKEN for secure image pushes to GitHub Container Registry.

items.title: Portainer Environment Management
items.description: Deploy Portainer as a lightweight management UI to visualize container health and manage remote Docker engines via an agent.

items.title: Watchtower Auto-Updates
items.description: Use the Watchtower container to automatically pull the latest images and restart containers when a new version is pushed to the registry.

items.title: Container Resource Monitoring
items.description: Deploy cAdvisor to collect real-time resource usage and performance characteristics of running containers for Prometheus scraping.

pro_tips: Use 'docker buildx' to create multi-platform images (amd64/arm64) with a single command for M1/M2 Mac and Linux server compatibility.
Never store secrets in Dockerfile 'ENV' instructions; use Docker Secrets or mount sensitive files at runtime to prevent leakages in image history.
Combine 'apt-get update' and 'apt-get install' in a single 'RUN' instruction followed by 'rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*' to keep layers small.
Use the '--init' flag when running containers via CLI if you haven't included an init system like Tini in your Dockerfile.
Leverage 'docker-compose config' to validate your YAML syntax and view the merged configuration of multiple override files before deployment.
